BalancedScorecard: Updating the calculation methodology will allow for a more accurate representation of the RFT metric within EM DS.
CurrentResponse:
The deviation is being managed through the standard DMAIC process with appropriate corrective actions identified.
This is not a repeat occurrence.
The RFT calculation methodology will be discussed at the next Quality Council meeting with a proposal to count all process steps at Aplora to align with other DS CMOs.
DIA: EM DS Quality Council to confirm the proposed RFT calculation approach. Need input from Vitor Antonio and Dilawar Mansoori.
NextSteps: Discuss and confirm the RFT calculation approach. Check with the team for extra next steps.
PatientImpact: There is no impact to patients.
Recommendation: Align Aplora RFT calculation methodology with other DS CMOs to ensure consistent reporting.
Situation:
Due to variations in how RFT is calculated across suppliers, a single deviation can lead to the DS RFT metric presenting as red.
For example, in January 2028 there was one final batch from Aplora with a single deviation, resulting in 0% RFT.

BalancedScorecard: The Red status and delays impact project timelines, cost, and risk metrics on the balanced scorecard, requiring mitigation to maintain overall program objectives.
CurrentResponse: Three major deviations were raised during DP PPQ 4 manufacture/fill: incorrect input component used, infeed arm collision, and post-use GIT test failure. PQTS+ review of PPQ 4 validity determination is ongoing, and Patheon impact assessment is in progress with a formal memo expected by June 26. AVI PPQ was completed in Dec 2025, and recipe optimization is in progress. Registration stability samples have been set down, and release testing of the original PPQ campaign was completed in Dec 2025. PPQ 4 release results are delayed due to deviation assessment. A replan of the submission target is required, with an approximate delay of 6-8 weeks if PPQ 4 deviation determines no impact.
DIA: EM QA and MSAT DP to confirm PPQ 4 validity acceptance decision and agree on mitigation actions for submission readiness.
DIATaskName: DIA - Nucala 2nd Source Program - Patheon GRV Tech Transfer
KPIName: Nucala 2nd Source Program - Patheon GRV Tech Transfer
NextSteps: Complete AVI of DP PPQ 4 pending deviation impact assessment; finalize validity acceptance decision by EM QA and MSAT DP (target June 26); continue US submission authoring and track readiness for Oct 2026 submission, contingent on PPQ acceptance and results availability.
PatientImpact: There is no direct patient impact at this stage; delays primarily affect submission timelines and project readiness.
Recommendation: Proceed with deviation impact assessment and validity determination for PPQ 4, and align on revised submission timelines to minimize overall program risk.
Situation: Technical transfer to Patheon is in progress. The overall status is Red due to a major quality event involving rogue input material controls at Patheon Greenville during PPQ 4 drug product filling activity, where an incorrect syringe component was introduced. Full impact to schedule, cost, and risk will be realized following deviation impact assessment and validity determination.
